surfaces.note: sidebar (#0E1220) and card (#12203A) are dark blue. background (#16141D) and input (#221E34) remain dark purple-near-black. Luminance ordering preserved: sidebar < background < card < input. card and input are very close in luminance (0.01467 vs 0.01517) — intentional; they occupy the same visual tier.

text.primary.wcag: AA 4.5:1 normal text — all surfaces pass with very high margin

text.secondary.wcag: AA 4.5:1 normal text — all surfaces pass with strong margin
text.secondary.note: Hot pink-family secondary text — model names and thread titles. Lighter than borders.default (#FF2070) so it reads as text rather than chrome.

text.muted.wcag: AA 4.5:1 normal text — all surfaces pass with strong margin
text.muted.note: Desaturated teal — shifted from muted purple #A090C0 to bring teal presence to timestamps and placeholder text. Luminance is lower than borders.strong (#2EE4B9) and model-gpt (#2EE4B9) — reads as dim/secondary, not competing with those tokens. Teal family is consistent: borders.strong, focusRing, model-gpt, and now text.muted all use the same hue family, with text.muted desaturated and darker to signal secondary hierarchy.

text.inverse.wcag: AA 4.5:1 — passes on all accent-colored button and badge surfaces

borders.default.wcag: borders.default is a structural border (hot pink card edges), not text. WCAG SC 1.4.3 applies to text and images of text only. The 4.40:1 on card and 4.37:1 on input are below the 4.5:1 text threshold but above the 3:1 SC 1.4.11 UI component threshold. Card borders appear against card surfaces, input outlines appear against input surfaces — the borderline cases are on their matching surfaces. Sidebar and background pass 4.5:1.
borders.default.note: The card surface shift from purple #1E1B2E to dark blue #12203A reduced borders.default contrast on card from 4.55:1 to 4.40:1 — the blue surface has marginally lower luminance delta with hot pink than the former purple. Still above the SC 1.4.11 3:1 UI component threshold. Deliberate and documented.

borders.subtle.wcag: Exempt — decorative structural separator, not text or interactive control. Near-invisible hairline divider. Consistent with all other themes where borders.subtle is intentionally low-contrast.

borders.strong.wcag: AA — all surfaces pass with very high margin
borders.strong.note: Night Drive teal. Contrast on new dark blue card is 9.98:1 — essentially unchanged from v2's 10.32:1 on purple card, as the blue and purple card values share similar luminance.

interactive.focusRing.ratios: Same as borders.strong — 9.91:1 worst case (on input). AA PASS.
interactive.focusRing.note: Teal focus ring is clearly visible on all surfaces including the new dark blue sidebar and card.

accents.model-claude.wcag: AA — all surfaces pass. Amber identity unchanged.

semantic.info.wcag: AA — all surfaces pass. 4.50:1 on input meets AA exactly (unchanged from v2). 4.54:1 on card passes with a slim margin.
semantic.info.note: Thinnest margin in the audit. If semantic.info is ever needed as body text on the input surface, bump to #D060FF (5.22:1 worst case). For current use (streaming indicator), 4.50:1 is sufficient.

_design_notes.revision: Issue #96 v3 — polish pass on v2. Two changes: teal expansion (text.muted, shadow.sm) and dark blue surfaces (sidebar, card).
_design_notes.surface_philosophy: sidebar (#0E1220) and card (#12203A) shift to dark blue. background (#16141D) and input (#221E34) remain dark purple-near-black. The split palette creates structural depth: the outer frame (sidebar navigation, content cards) reads as dark navy while the content zone (main background, input field) retains the moody purple character established in v2. Progressive luminance preserved: sidebar (0.00630) < background (0.00760) < card (0.01467) < input (0.01517).
_design_notes.surface_shift_rationale: Sidebar and card are the natural candidates for the blue shift because they are visually distinct frame regions — the sidebar is the navigation chrome and card surfaces are where model responses live. Shifting these to dark blue grounds the structural frame in cool techno-navy and makes the purple background/input surfaces feel warmer by contrast. Keeping background and input in the same purple family maintains coherence in the content zone where most reading happens. Effect: structural frame in dark blue, content zone in moody purple — two clearly different regions, one palette.
_design_notes.teal_expansion: text.muted shifts from muted purple #A090C0 to desaturated teal #6BBFB8. Teal is now present at every level: borders.strong (#2EE4B9), focusRing (#2EE4B9), model-gpt (#2EE4B9), shadow.sm (rgba teal layer), shadow.lg (rgba teal layer), text.muted (#6BBFB8). #6BBFB8 is desaturated and lower-luminance than #2EE4B9 — reads as dim/secondary, not as an accent. Timestamps, placeholder text, and helper labels carry a cool teal undertone consistent with the Night Drive palette.
_design_notes.shadow_sm_update: shadow.sm updated from pure pink glow (rgba(255,32,112,0.45)) to pink-dominant dual-channel: 0 0 8px rgba(255,32,112,0.35), 0 0 4px rgba(46,228,185,0.20). Pink opacity reduced from 0.45 to 0.35 to leave room for the teal layer without increasing total glow intensity. Pink remains the dominant read at this elevation; teal adds depth. shadow.md and shadow.lg unchanged.
_design_notes.hot_pink_as_dominant_accent: Hot pink (#FF2070) remains the primary structural accent. borders.default on the new dark blue card surface is 4.40:1 — structural chrome exemption applies (SC 1.4.11, 3:1 threshold for UI components). Unchanged reasoning from v2.
_design_notes.teal_as_counterpoint: Teal is now a full-palette presence: borders.strong, focusRing, model-gpt, shadow.sm (minor layer), shadow.lg (secondary layer), text.muted. The hot-pink/teal chromatic engine runs from structural borders through glows to secondary text.
_design_notes.night_drive_treatment: Unchanged from v2. Dark-purple-near-black content zone + hot-pink/teal pairing are the essential mood anchors. Dark blue frame surfaces add a new dimension: navy outer shell, purple interior warmth. Consistent with nocturnal synthwave aesthetics.
